text: The surgeon performs a single-incision laparoscopic total colectomy on a 39-year-old female with ulcerative colitis. The procedure begins with excising a disc-shaped section of skin in the left lower quadrant, followed by muscle-splitting entry into the abdomen. A 12mm and two 5mm trocars are inserted. Dissection starts at the right colon, with identification and ligation of the ileocolic pedicle. Medial to lateral dissection is performed, preserving the duodenum. The ascending colon is mobilized, and a clockwise right-to-left total colectomy is completed. The hepatocolic ligament is divided, and the transverse colon is mobilized by dividing the mesocolon and greater omentum. After taking down the splenic flexure, the left branch of the middle colic artery is ligated, leaving the inferior mesenteric artery intact. The rectosigmoid junction is stapled, and the colon is exteriorized through the ileostomy site. The ileostomy is matured, and the rectal stump remains in the pelvis without implantation.

Six months post-colectomy, the surgeon performs a circumferential incision around the stoma site in the left lower quadrant. The loop ileostomy is released, temporarily sealed with a trio-viral stick, and returned to the abdomen.

The surgeon achieves pneumoperitoneum using a single port access device. The inferior mesenteric artery is ligated at the superior artery level. Dissection is performed through the avascular plane posterior to the mesorectum, extending down to the levator muscles. Once the levator muscles are reached, dissection continues bilaterally. Anterior mobilization is performed, followed by a digital rectal exam to confirm the dissection level. The laparoscopic GIA-XVI stapler is fired twice to complete the procedure.

A 15 to 20 centimeter ileal pouch is constructed using ILA-100 and TA-13 staplers. A leak test is performed on the pouch, showing no leakage. An EEA-XXVIII anvil is inserted into the pouch, and under laparoscopic view, an intracorporeal anastomosis is created. A poshoscopy is performed to inspect the anastomosis.

A loop of bowel proximal to the ileal pouch is identified and exteriorized through the left lower quadrant ileostomy site. The ileostomy is matured, and the intact specimen is displayed. The video concludes with the patient's postoperative recovery being uneventful.
